what_it_does: Synthesizes the evidence base and reuse inventory for building a registry-generation chain that grows canonical ui:// address entries from mockup elements; it documents the measured gap, existing engine primitives to reuse, net-new components, reuse guards, and a parallel-lane work split.
observations: Gap measured: 23 mockups, 82 registered ui:// addresses, 102 data-ui-ref attributes, and one mockup (C1-inbox) has 131 elements with only 3 addressed.
Registry entry structure is described as {represents, code, howto{what/can-do/how-to-change}, capabilities}.

Reuses engine primitives: run_items, run_reduce with cluster mode, run_role, save_cascade/run_cascade/list_cascades, screen_reader role, design/_system/parse.py, addresses.json, corpus-meta.json, governance inbox/surface, refcheck.py, and a feature/function inventory.
Net-new components are small: register_element role, an EXTRACT extension to parse.py, a cascade definition, a proposal write-back path, and a CONFIRM jury.
Explicit reuse-not-parallel guards: no second registry, no second parser, no hand-orchestration, operator-only write approval, no-fiction capability claims, and introspective provenance on proposals.
Work is split into MINE (guided-review), COGNITION (parallel loop), and JOINT lanes.
Provenance taxonomy used: Observed(file), Verified-by-use, Inferred.
File is grounded on main as of 2026-06-09.
standouts: The chain is framed as almost entirely reuse; net-new is one role, one parser pass, one proposal write-back, and one confirm jury.
run_items is highlighted as the axis-inversion primitive that maps one role over a corpus of candidate elements.
The governance inbox/surface is the mandatory operator-approval gate before registry mutation.
The parallel-loop split assigns surface/registry round-trip work to one lane and engine/role depth to another.
conventions: Markdown with numbered sections and a provenance taxonomy.
Reuse-before-build stance with explicit guards against parallel registries, parsers, and chain-runners.
Registry-as-truth: pipeline declared as data via saved cascades rather than hardcoded orchestration.
Separation of concerns into MINE/COGNITION/JOINT responsibility lanes.
Operator-only floor for writing to the canonical registry.
concerns: Project-specific abbreviations (V-A, V-B, RHM) appear without definition in this file.
A specific operator name ('Tim') is named as the sole approval gate, which may be brittle.
The 'meaningful' filter for candidate elements is mentioned but not specified.
Confidence threshold and exact mechanics of the CONFIRM jury are not specified here.
Fallback behavior for delayed or rejected operator approval is not described.
notes: A build-prep research synthesis that maps existing engine primitives onto a registry-generation pipeline and partitions the remaining work between two parallel loops.
questions: What exactly qualifies a mockup element as 'meaningful' for extraction?
What is the confidence threshold and composition of the CONFIRM jury?
How are cluster-dedup parameters tuned for cross-mockup duplicate detection?
What is the precise schema of the candidate-element unit emitted by parse.py?
What happens if operator approval is delayed or rejected?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to serve as the evidence base for the V-A loop and guide building the registry-generation chain; the file matches that purpose by inventorying gaps, existing primitives, net-new work, guards, and lane split.
apparent_themes: Corpus-scale axis inversion (one role applied to many units).
intention_signals: It is trying to bootstrap a systematic, operator-approved registry-growth pipeline for UI mockup elements by reusing existing swarm engine primitives, keeping the design-system registry as the single source of truth, and splitting implementation between a surface/registry round-trip lane and a cognition/role-engine lane.
novelty: The explicit use of run_items to apply one role across a corpus of candidate UI elements, combined with a governance-surface approval gate before mutating the canonical address registry, and a parallel-loop work split between guided-review and cognition.
contribution: It contributes a scoped build plan and reuse inventory that anchors the registry-generation chain in existing engine primitives, defines the operator-approval boundary, and partitions net-new work into discrete, lane-owned pieces.
summary_for_embedding: Research synthesis documenting the evidence base, reuse inventory, net-new components, governance guards, and parallel-lane split for a registry-generation chain that grows the canonical ui:// address registry from mockup elements.
intention_for_embedding: To guide construction of a re-runnable, operator-approved pipeline that fills the address-registry gap by extracting candidate elements, generating dossiers, deduplicating across mockups, confirming code references, and surfacing proposals for approval.
